top_stories.label: Top Stories
top_stories.ai_summary: The big news today is diplomatic: President Trump says a deal with Iran to end the war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz is "largely negotiated," though Iran is publicly disputing the terms. Tragedy struck in China where a coal mine gas explosion in Shanxi killed at least 90 people, the country's worst mining disaster in over a decade. Washington has also moved to force most green-card applicants to leave the U.S. and apply from abroad — a sweeping change affecting roughly 600,000 people a year. On a lighter note, Romanian director Cristian Mungiu won his second Palme d'Or at Cannes, and SpaceX's upgraded Starship completed a full test flight before its planned splashdown explosion in the Indian Ocean.

stories.headline: Trump says Iran war deal and Strait of Hormuz reopening 'largely negotiated'
stories.summary: President Trump announced on Saturday that he has spoken with leaders of Saudi Arabia, the UAE, Qatar, Pakistan, Turkey, Egypt, Jordan, Bahrain and Israel about a framework deal to formally end the Iran war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Final details are still being negotiated. However, Iran's semi-official Fars news agency contradicted the announcement, insisting the strait will remain under Iran's management.

stories.headline: China coal mine gas explosion kills 90 in worst disaster in over a decade
stories.summary: A gas explosion at the Liushenyu coal mine in Shanxi province on Friday evening killed at least 90 people out of 247 workers on duty. The mine had been flagged in 2024 for high gas content. Investigators say blueprints provided by the mine operator did not match the actual layout, hampering rescue efforts. China's State Council has launched an investigation.

stories.headline: Trump administration forces most green-card applicants to leave U.S. and apply abroad
stories.summary: In a memo issued May 21 and made public on May 23, USCIS said green cards will be issued from within the U.S. only in 'extraordinary circumstances'. The policy reverses more than 50 years of practice and affects roughly 600,000 people each year, including spouses of U.S. citizens, work-visa holders, students, and asylum seekers. Immigration advocates have already begun pushing back.

stories.headline: Romanian director Cristian Mungiu wins second Palme d'Or for 'Fjord'
stories.summary: Cristian Mungiu became only the 11th director ever to win the Palme d'Or twice, taking the top prize at Cannes for 'Fjord' — a culture-war drama starring Sebastian Stan and Renate Reinsve about a Romanian family clashing with Norwegian authorities over child discipline. His first win was for '4 Months, 3 Weeks and 2 Days' 19 years ago. Distributor Neon now holds U.S. rights to the Palme d'Or winner for the seventh year running.

stories.headline: SpaceX Starship completes test flight, explodes on planned splashdown
stories.summary: SpaceX launched its upgraded Starship V3 from Starbase, Texas on Friday. The roughly hour-long flight deployed mock Starlink satellites and gathered re-entry data before the vehicle's planned breakup after splashdown in the Indian Ocean. Both rocket stages experienced engine failures during the mission, but SpaceX says key objectives were met.

singapore.label: Singapore
singapore.ai_summary: A quiet Saturday in Singapore with mostly bread-and-butter news. The big story for drivers is the latest COE bidding result — premiums hit fresh highs, with Cat E breaching $130k. Two locals previously on the MV Hondius cruise ship remain in isolation at NCID awaiting hantavirus test results. The fortnight's weather outlook says expect afternoon thundery showers most days, with daily highs of 33–34°C. A fatal cycling accident in Pasir Ris and an HDB/LTA promise of earlier bus services for new BTO estates round out the local headlines.

stories.headline: COE prices surge again in May second bidding — Cat E hits $130,000
stories.summary: The 20 May COE bidding closed with Category B at $129,501, Category C at an all-time high of $92,223, and Category E at $130,000. Only Category A eased slightly to $124,229. Demand for vehicles remains resilient despite record-high ownership costs as buyers rush to lock in COEs amid limited supply.

stories.headline: Two Singapore residents from MV Hondius isolated at NCID, hantavirus test results pending
stories.summary: The Communicable Diseases Agency confirmed that two Singapore residents who were aboard the MV Hondius — the Dutch cruise ship at the centre of the Andes hantavirus cluster — are being tested at NCID. Both will be quarantined for 30 days from last exposure and monitored for a further 15 days. CDA says risk to the wider public is low.

stories.headline: Weather outlook: thundery showers most afternoons, highs of 33–34°C
stories.summary: For the second fortnight of May the Meteorological Service expects short-duration thundery showers over parts of the island on most afternoons, occasionally lasting into the evening. Sumatra squalls may also bring widespread thundery showers and gusty winds in the pre-dawn hours on a few days. Pack an umbrella for the evening commute.

stories.headline: Cyclist, 35, dies in Pasir Ris accident with tipper truck
stories.summary: A 35-year-old cyclist was killed on Saturday afternoon after a collision with a tipper truck in Pasir Ris. Police are investigating. The death adds to a string of fatal road incidents involving cyclists and heavy vehicles in recent months.

stories.headline: HDB and LTA to roll out earlier bus services for new large-scale BTO estates
stories.summary: Residents moving into new large-scale BTO estates will get earlier access to cooked food, groceries, childcare and bus services — up to three months sooner than before. HDB and LTA will plan at least one bus service operational in tandem with the first batch of key collection, connecting residents to MRT stations and interchanges from day one.

wars.label: Wars & Conflicts
wars.ai_summary: A complicated weekend across the war map. In Ukraine, Russia unleashed one of its largest combined missile-and-drone strikes of the year on Kyiv, killing at least two and injuring 83. In the Gulf, Trump claims a framework deal to end the Iran war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz is nearly finished, but Tehran disputes the terms publicly — so brinkmanship continues. The Israel–Lebanon ceasefire was extended 45 days but Israeli strikes are still hitting southern Lebanon, killing six in Harouf, including paramedics. Net: diplomacy and bombs, side by side.

theatres.name: Russia–Ukraine War
theatres.status: Active — massive overnight strike on Kyiv

timeline.event: Russia launches full-scale invasion of Ukraine
Ukraine launches Kursk cross-border offensive into Russian territory
Front lines remain largely static through autumn and winter; war of attrition deepens
Russia loses a net 29 square miles of Ukrainian territory in the week of May 12–19

Massive overnight strike on Kyiv: 549 drones and 55 missiles intercepted by air defences; 2 dead, 83 injured. Sumy region: 1 dead, 20+ injured including a 12-year-old child. Russian combat losses now estimated at 1,355,920 personnel since 2022.

theatres.name: US/Israel–Iran (Strait of Hormuz)
theatres.status: Ceasefire holding but strait still effectively closed; framework deal in flux

timeline.event: US and Israel launch joint air war on Iran; Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei assassinated
Iran declares the Strait of Hormuz 'closed'; threatens any ship that tries to transit. Tanker traffic collapses to near zero.
US and Iran agree on a ceasefire that also includes Israel after five weeks of fighting
US says offensive phase is over but Tehran refuses to give up control of Hormuz

Trump announces a deal to formally end the war and reopen Hormuz is 'largely negotiated', after calls with Saudi Arabia, UAE, Qatar, Pakistan, Turkey, Egypt, Jordan, Bahrain and Israel. Iran's Fars news agency contradicts him, saying Tehran will continue to manage the waterway.

theatres.name: Israel–Lebanon / Gaza
theatres.status: Ceasefire extended 45 days; Israeli strikes continue in south Lebanon

timeline.event: Current round of fighting between Israel and Hezbollah breaks out
Initial Israel–Lebanon ceasefire takes effect, brokered by the US — a 10-day truce
Trump announces three-week extension of the ceasefire
Ceasefire extended another 45 days. Same day, Israeli airstrike on Harouf, southern Lebanon, kills 6 including 3 paramedics

Cumulative casualties since 2 March: 2,491 killed and 7,719 wounded according to Lebanon's Health Ministry. Hezbollah calls the ceasefire extension 'meaningless'; Israeli forces remain in a so-called 'yellow line' inside southern Lebanon.

hantavirus.label: Hantavirus Outbreak
hantavirus.ai_summary: The MV Hondius hantavirus cluster looks contained but not closed. No new cases or deaths since the last update — the count stays at 11 (9 confirmed, 2 probable, 3 deaths) across 23 countries. The ship is now docked in Rotterdam for sanitation. Two Singapore residents who were aboard remain in isolation at NCID awaiting test results. ECDC says risk to the wider public is very low, but more cases could surface because Andes virus has a long incubation period and has the unusual ability to spread person-to-person.

stories.headline: MV Hondius hantavirus count holds at 11 cases, 3 deaths — ship docked in Rotterdam
stories.summary: As of 21 May, no new cases or deaths have been reported since the previous update. The total stands at 9 confirmed and 2 probable cases, with 3 deaths (2 of them confirmed Andes hantavirus). The Dutch-flagged cruise ship has been docked in Rotterdam since 18 May undergoing sanitation. Passengers and crew come from 23 countries including 9 in the EU/EEA.

stories.headline: Two Singapore residents from MV Hondius isolated at NCID, tests pending
stories.summary: CDA Singapore was notified on 4 and 5 May that two residents had been aboard the MV Hondius. Both have been isolated at the National Centre for Infectious Diseases and tested for hantavirus, with results still pending. They will be quarantined for 30 days from their last exposure and monitored by phone for a further 15 days. CDA says public risk remains low.

stories.headline: WHO and ECDC: more cases possible due to long incubation period
stories.summary: Both WHO and ECDC continue to assess the risk to the general public as very low, but warn that additional cases may yet appear after former passengers and crew have returned home. Andes virus has a long incubation period and is the only known hantavirus that spreads from person to person. Past outbreaks have had case-fatality rates of 20–40%.

stories.headline: WHO declares DR Congo / Uganda Ebola outbreak a Public Health Emergency of International Concern
stories.summary: On 17 May the WHO declared the Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak in the DRC and Uganda a PHEIC. As of 22 May, 836 suspected cases and at least 186 deaths have been reported, with the outbreak now in North Kivu (Butembo and Goma) beyond initial Ituri Province cases. Bundibugyo virus has no licensed vaccine or therapeutic — a serious concern as the existing Ebola vaccine ERVEBO only protects against a different species.

stats.ship_status: Docked in Rotterdam since 18 May, sanitation ongoing

cybersecurity.label: CyberSecurity
cybersecurity.ai_summary: Critical-bug overload this cycle. Cisco patched a CVSS 10.0 authentication bypass in Secure Workload and is dealing with another 10.0 SD-WAN flaw already exploited in the wild. cPanel admins are getting hit hardest: a CVSS 9.8 auth bypass is being weaponised within 24 hours of disclosure by Mirai variants and a new ransomware called Sorry. Add Apache HTTP/2 (DoS + potential RCE), Palo Alto PAN-OS (under active exploitation), an Ollama memory leak hitting 300,000+ servers, and CISA piling Langflow and Trend Micro Apex One onto its KEV list — and it is a long patching weekend for blue teams.

stories.headline: Cisco Secure Workload — maximum-severity REST API authentication bypass
stories.summary: An unauthenticated remote attacker can send a crafted HTTP request to an internal REST API endpoint and instantly gain Site Admin privileges across tenant boundaries. Affects Secure Workload in both SaaS and on-prem; no workaround. Patch to 4.0.3.17 (v4.0) or 3.10.8.3 (v3.10); v3.9 and earlier must migrate. Cisco found the flaw via internal testing — no public exploitation yet.

stories.headline: Cisco SD-WAN Controller — actively exploited authentication bypass
stories.summary: A critical authentication bypass in Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller was exploited in zero-day attacks in May, allowing attackers to gain administrative privileges on compromised devices. CISA has added it to the Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalogue. Patch immediately if you run SD-WAN Controller.

stories.headline: cPanel auth bypass weaponised within 24 hours — Mirai variants and 'Sorry' ransomware deployed
stories.summary: A critical authentication bypass in cPanel and WHM is being weaponised by multiple third parties within 24 hours of public disclosure. Attackers are deploying Mirai botnet variants and a new ransomware strain called 'Sorry' on government and MSP networks. Patch your WHM/cPanel installations immediately.

stories.headline: Apache HTTP/2 double-free flaw enables DoS and potential RCE
stories.summary: Apache has patched a double-free vulnerability in HTTP/2 affecting Apache HTTP Server 2.4.66. The flaw enables denial-of-service and potential remote code execution. Upgrade to the fixed version as soon as your change window allows — HTTP/2 is widely enabled by default on production web servers.

stories.headline: Ollama memory leak hits 300,000+ servers globally
stories.summary: A critical out-of-bounds read vulnerability in Ollama lets a remote, unauthenticated attacker leak the entire process memory — potentially exposing API keys, model weights, and user prompts. The flaw likely affects more than 300,000 internet-exposed Ollama servers worldwide.

stories.headline: Palo Alto PAN-OS flaw under active exploitation
stories.summary: A vulnerability in Palo Alto Networks' PAN-OS is being actively exploited in the wild and enables remote code execution. Network defenders running PAN-OS firewalls should review Palo Alto's advisory and apply the patch out-of-band if necessary.

stories.headline: CISA adds Langflow and Trend Micro Apex One flaws to KEV catalogue
stories.summary: On 21 May CISA added two new entries to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalogue: an origin-validation error in Langflow (CVE-2025-34291) and a directory-traversal flaw in Trend Micro Apex One On-Premise (CVE-2026-34926). Federal agencies must remediate by CISA's deadline; private operators should patch the same.

stories.headline: Windows BitLocker zero-day exposes protected drives — public PoC released
stories.summary: A zero-day affecting Windows BitLocker allows attackers with physical access to read protected drives. A proof-of-concept exploit is now public, raising the urgency for organisations relying on BitLocker for disk encryption on laptops to evaluate compensating controls.
